differentials.name: Bouveret syndrome
differentials.raw: Bouveret syndrome (= gallstone erodes into duodenum leading to duodenal obstruction)

context: Etiology: (a)   in 80-95% cystic duct obstruction by impacted calculus; 85% disimpact spontaneously if stone < 3 mm (b)  in 10% acalculous cholecystitis Acute Calculous Cholecystitis Pathogenesis:   chemical irritation from concentrated bile, bacterial infection, reflux of pancreatic secretions Age peak:   5th-6th decade; M÷F = 1÷3 Associated with:   choledocholithiasis (15-25%) ◊  5% of patients presenting to ED have acute cholecystitis! biliary colic usually < 6 hours) US (40-97% sensitive, 64-100% specific, 92% PPV, 95% NPV): √  ± GB wall thickening > 3 mm (45-72% sensitive, 76-88% specific
